Reason_for_Visit.summary: Newly diagnosed Stage 2 low grade IDC of the right breast, s/p right partial mastectomy, to discuss systemic therapy options

Clinical_Findings.findings: Tumor size: 1.9 x 1.3 x 1.6 cm, 1.7 x 1.5 cm, 2.3 cm; Margins: widely negative; Node status: 1 positive lymph node, 0.04 cm; Grade: G1 IDC, G2 IDC, nuclear G1 DCIS

Treatment_Changes.recent_changes: Patient started letrozole, switched from no radiation to starting letrozole immediately
supportive_meds: ondansetron (ZOFRAN), oxyCODONE-acetaminophen (PERCOCET), SUMAtriptan (IMITREX), lidocaine (PF) (XYLOCAINE), lidocaine (XYLOCAINE URO-*****)

Treatment_Goals.response_assessment: The patient has 1 positive lymph node. The other + LN was direct extension. Thus I believe she will derive the greatest benefit from adjuvant endocrine therapy with letrozole.
Treatment_Goals.current_meds: ascorbic acid, vitamin C, baclofen, cyclobenzaprine, ergocalciferol, vitamin D2, estradiol, gabapentin, ibuprofen, levothyroxine, modafinil, nitrofurantoin, ondansetron, oxyCODONE-acetaminophen, SUMAtriptan, teriflunomide, levofloxacin, lidocaine, onabotulinumtoxinA

Response_Assessment.response_assessment: The patient has 1 positive lymph node. The other + LN was direct extension. Thus I believe she will derive the greatest benefit from adjuvant endocrine therapy with letrozole.
Response_Assessment.current_meds: ascorbic acid, baclofen, cyclobenzaprine, ergocalciferol, estradiol, gabapentin, ibuprofen, levothyroxine, modafinil, nitrofurantoin, ondansetron, oxyCODONE-acetaminophen, SUMAtriptan, teriflunomide, letrozole, levofloxacin, lidocaine, onabotulinumtoxinA

hormonal therapy.Short term side_effects_discussed: hot flashes, arthralgias, bone pain, hair thinning, dryness

Procedure_Plan.procedure_plan: PT referral, start letrozole immediately if no radiation is planned

Genetic_Testing_Plan.genetic_testing_plan: We could send an FISH or Oncotype DX

follow_up_next_visit.response_assessment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.
